description_long: This object is a compact, handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, with a robust, military-style design. The body is primarily olive green with a darker gray lower section, and it features a long, black flexible antenna extending from the top. The front face displays a numeric keypad, a small display screen, and several physical buttons for operation, while the top has two rotary knobs likely for power/volume and channel selection. The sides are relatively plain, with subtle texturing for grip and possibly a push-to-talk button on one side. The overall construction appears rugged, suitable for outdoor or tactical urban use, and the device is designed to be easily portable and operated with one hand.
description: A rugged, olive green handheld walkie-talkie with a long black antenna, numeric keypad, and rotary knobs. Designed for portable communication in urban or tactical environments.
description_view_0: Side view showing the slim profile, antenna, and subtle grip texture.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the keypad, display screen, and control buttons, as well as the two rotary knobs on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other slim edge and antenna.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain back panel with a single visible button or latch and the two rotary knobs on top.
